extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) both true.

rec_drug_a: Only r2 (priority 50) sets it true, and only when all five flags hold (elderly AND peptic_ulcer AND hypertension AND g6pd_deficiency AND immunosuppressed). No rule sets rec_drug_a false. So rec_drug_a = true iff exactly that five-flag conjunction holds.

rec_drug_b: r5 (priority 50) sets true when elderly; r6 (priority 90) sets false when the same five-flag conjunction holds; default false.

Critical interaction: rec_drug_a becomes true ONLY in the five-flag state. In that exact state, both r5 (elderly true, sets b:=true, prio 50) and r6 (all five true, sets b:=false, prio 90) match output rec_drug_b. Per priority conflict resolution, the highest-priority matching rule wins: r6 (90) beats r5 (50), so rec_drug_b = false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b is forced false by the override rule r6.

The override condition of r6 is identical to (and thus fully covers) the trigger condition of r2, so the dangerous combination is unreachable. The other inputs (hyperkalemia, polypharmacy, diabetes) only influence rec_drug_c, which is irrelevant to this property. Therefore no input assignment yields rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b. The property holds.
